name: Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop
name_original: Balazote — circular por la Via Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz
description: This easy 10.8-mile mountain biking loop on the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway near Balazote offers a scenic ride through changing landscapes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Balazote offers a diverse range of terrain, from the gentle gradients of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz to the more challenging ascents within the Sierra de Alcaraz region. The area features agricultural plains, forest tracks, and limestone rock formations, providing varied landscapes for cyclists. Routes often follow old railway lines, incorporating tunnels and viaducts. This region caters to mountain bikers seeking both relaxed rides and demanding climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Balazote The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop, an easy 10.8 miles (17 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic ride along the historic greenway. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tunnel – Masegoso River Viaduct loop from Balazote, a difficult 63.3 miles (102 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain and features a river viaduct. Local mountain bikers also love the Masegoso River Viaduct – Rest area loop from Balazote, a 35.6 miles (57 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Balazote is defined by greenways, agricultural plains, and the more rugged terrain of the Sierra de Alcaraz. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Balazote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Balazote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Balazote?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Balazote, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Balazote?
A: The terrain around Balazote is quite varied. You'll find gentle gradients along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1003575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line through agricultural plains. As you venture towards the Sierra de Alcaraz, the terrain becomes more rugged, featuring forest tracks, challenging ascents, and limestone rock formations.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy mountain bike trails around Balazote. A great option for a relaxed ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1216834710/sierra-de-alcaraz-greenway-balazote-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 17.4 km route takes about 1 hour 15 minutes to complete and offers a scenic journey along the historic greenway, perfect for those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel – Masegoso River Viaduct loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 101.9 km route with significant elevation gain. This trail will test your fitness and skills, taking you through varied landscapes and featuring the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masegoso River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Balazote?
A: The mountain biking trails around Balazote are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from the accessible greenways to the more demanding routes in the Sierra de Alcaraz, and the unique experience of cycling along old railway lines with tunnels and viaducts.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Balazote?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Balazote are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masegoso River Viaduct – Rest area loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 57.2 km trail that offers a circular journey through varied terrain, typically completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the trails often incorporate interesting features. Along the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, you can encounter historical railway infrastructure like tunnels and viaducts, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masegoso River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by rest areas such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Santanas Rest Area&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient stops during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Balazote?
A: The best time to visit Balazote for mountain biking is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, while winters might bring cooler temperatures, though the region generally experiences a Mediterranean climate.

Q: Are there any routes that start from nearby towns like San Pedro or Pozuelo?
A: Yes, there are routes that begin from neighboring areas. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro – Casas de Lázaro loop from San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 43.3 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro – Detour from the route loop from Pozuelo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 44.8 km route.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in the region?
A: The region offers a diverse natural landscape. You'll ride through agricultural plains, especially along the Vía Verde. Further afield, towards the Sierra de Alcaraz, you can expect to see gigantic limestone rock walls, lush forests, and potentially glimpses of the karst landscape of Calar del Mundo. The area is also known for its rivers and spectacular views from higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific facilities or rest stops along the trails?
A: Yes, some routes, particularly those along the Vía Verde, feature disused train stations that can serve as informal rest stops. Additionally, dedicated facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Santanas Rest Area&lt;/a&gt; are available on certain loops, providing convenient places to pause and refresh during your ride.
tips: The track is ideal for enjoying the beautiful gravel roads, far-reaching, open views and beautiful traffic-free bridges.
This route can also be done with a gravel bike or even a road bike with 30-32mm tires. Some of the tunnels along the route have no lights and a bike light is recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop?
faqs.answer: The Balazote Loop is an easy mountain bike ride covering approximately 17.4 km. It typically takes around 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete, making it suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Balazote Loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Balazote, which serves as a recognized stopping point for the greenway. The area often includes a rest area with amenities like benches, tables, bike racks, and sometimes even a bike repair station.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The surface of the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway around Balazote is a mix of compacted earth, gravel, and tarmac. While generally flat, some sections may have less fine gravel. A mountain bike or hybrid bike with mixed tread tires is recommended for optimal performance on these varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Balazote Loop?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this route are generally spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter, making for a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular section are not detailed, greenways in Spain generally permit dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Sierra de Alcaraz Greenway: Balazote Loop. It is generally open for public use.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route follows the historic path of an old railway line, offering a unique blend of history and nature. You'll experience a transition from agricultural plains to more diverse, lightly mountainous landscapes as you approach the Sierra de Alcaraz. Notable features include tunnels (some unlit, so bring a light), viaducts, and disused train stations. The trail is part of the Alcaraz Greenway and the Greenway of the Sierra de Alcaraz.

faqs.question: Is the Balazote Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Balazote Loop is categorized as an 'easy' mountain bike ride. With a distance of 17.4 km and only 100 meters of elevation gain, it's well-suited for beginners and those looking for a relaxed ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this greenway?
faqs.answer: It's advisable to bring sufficient water and snacks, as services may be sparse in certain sections. Given the varied terrain, a mountain bike or hybrid bike with mixed tread tires is recommended. If you plan to explore tunnels, a light is essential as some are unlit.

faqs.question: Are there any other mountain biking options near Balazote?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the easy Balazote Loop, there are more challenging options. A 'Moderate Mountainbike loop from Balazote' is about 51.9 km with 370 meters of elevation gain, requiring good fitness. For experienced riders, the 'Casas de Lázaro – Tunnel loop from Balazote' is a hard 103 km ride with 1,050 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz. It also intersects with parts of the Sendero de los Serranos and other segments of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, including sections under construction or with detours.

waypoints.name: Greenway of the Sierra de Alcaraz
